title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated in evaluation and oversight rather than product releases. New work probes conflict-sensitive deployment failures, mechanistic interpretability of task errors, and privacy-preserving learning, while METR adds a fresh frontier-risk reporting snapshot and OpenAI pushes content provenance infrastructure.

items.title: METR publishes new Frontier Risk Report for February–March 2026
items.why_it_matters: Regular frontier-risk reporting improves external visibility into model capability and deployment hazards.

items.title: Study tests whether LLM deployments can worsen armed conflicts
items.why_it_matters: It targets a neglected deployment-risk domain where model outputs may amplify real-world harm.

items.title: OpenAI details content provenance work using Content Credentials, SynthID, and verification tooling
items.why_it_matters: Provenance standards and verification tools are central to media integrity and synthetic-content accountability.

items.title: Sparse-feature audit links GPT-2 IOI task failures to specific SAE activations
items.why_it_matters: Mechanistic audits can make model failures more diagnosable and interventions more targeted.

items.title: Lumberjack proposes stronger differentially private random forests
items.why_it_matters: Better privacy-utility tradeoffs could expand practical use of privacy-preserving ML on sensitive data.

items.title: Paper models adaptive distillation attacks and efficient defenses
items.why_it_matters: It sharpens the security tradeoff between useful model outputs and model-extraction risk.

items.title: Commercial AI chatbots evaluated as news intermediaries across emerging events
items.why_it_matters: News-facing chatbot accuracy affects public trust, misinformation exposure, and accountability for retrieval systems.

items.title: LCGuard introduces safeguards for latent KV-cache sharing in multi-agent systems
items.why_it_matters: Guarding hidden-agent communication addresses a growing attack surface in multi-agent architectures.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
